entry.title: Discord
entry.description: Discord is a free platform for real-time voice, video, and text communication, widely used for communities and teams.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier includes unlimited text, voice, and video channels with basic moderation and community features.
caveats: Advanced features like higher upload limits and HD video require paid Nitro subscription
Some moderation and security tools are limited

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Discord account at discord.com.
Create a new server for your team or community.
Set up text and voice channels as needed.
Invite members using the server invite link.
Configure roles and permissions for moderation.
entry.whenToUse: Use Discord when you need a free, easy-to-use platform for real-time group communication and community management.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Discord if you require enterprise compliance, advanced admin controls, or need to self-host your communication platform.
entry.body: Discord offers a robust free tier for real-time communication, ideal for communities and small teams needing chat, voice, and video features.

entry.title: DocsSearch
entry.description: DocsSearch provides fast, hosted search for documentation sites, powered by Algolia and free for open source projects.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free, fully managed search for open source documentation sites.
caveats: Not available for private or commercial projects
Approval required via application
billingRiskNotes: No billing for approved open source documentation projects

useCases: Add instant search to open source documentation
Enhance user navigation on docs sites
quickstartSteps: Apply for DocsSearch access with your open source documentation site.
Add the provided Algolia configuration to your documentation site.
Deploy your site and verify the search integration.
Monitor indexing and search performance via the Algolia dashboard.
entry.whenToUse: Use DocsSearch when you need fast, reliable search for a public open source documentation site.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if your project is private, commercial, or not documentation-focused, as DocsSearch is strictly for open source docs.
entry.body: DocsSearch by Algolia offers a free, managed search solution for open source documentation sites, enabling fast and relevant search with minimal setup.

entry.title: DynamicDocs
entry.description: DynamicDocs provides automated PDF and document generation from templates and data via API or web interface.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier allows limited monthly document generations with access to core features.
caveats: Branding watermark on free documents
No priority support

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free DynamicDocs account at advicement.io.
Create or upload a document template using the web interface.
Connect your data source or prepare sample data.
Use the API or web interface to generate documents from your template.
Download or share the generated PDFs.
entry.whenToUse: Use DynamicDocs when you need to automate PDF or document creation from templates and structured data.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require high-volume, unbranded documents or advanced customization beyond the free tier limits.
entry.body: DynamicDocs is a user-friendly platform for automating document and PDF generation from templates, suitable for small teams and startups needing basic workflows.

entry.title: GitBook
entry.description: GitBook is a collaborative documentation platform for teams to create, edit, and publish content online.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan for individuals and small teams with limited spaces and members.
caveats: Custom domains and advanced integrations require paid plans
Limited analytics

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free GitBook account.
Create a new space for your documentation.
Invite team members (up to 5 total).
Start writing and organizing content using the editor.
Publish your documentation as public or private.
entry.whenToUse: Use GitBook when you need a simple, collaborative platform for team or project documentation with easy publishing.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced customization, custom domains, or large team support without upgrading to a paid plan.
entry.body: GitBook offers a user-friendly platform for creating and sharing documentation, ideal for small teams and open source projects needing collaborative editing and publishing.

entry.title: Gitter.im
entry.description: Gitter.im is a free chat and networking platform for developers and open source communities.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited public rooms and messages for individuals and open source communities.
caveats: Private rooms are not included in the free tier
No advanced moderation or enterprise features

quickstartSteps: Go to https://gitter.im/ and sign up with your GitHub, GitLab, or Twitter account.
Authorize Gitter to access your account if prompted.
Create or join a public chat room relevant to your project or interest.
Invite collaborators or community members to your room.
Start chatting and sharing resources.
entry.whenToUse: Use Gitter.im for open source project discussions or public developer communities needing persistent, searchable chat rooms.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need private rooms, enterprise features, or advanced moderation; consider alternatives for confidential or business-critical communication.
entry.body: Gitter.im provides free, persistent chat rooms for open source projects and developer communities, supporting public collaboration with GitHub and GitLab integration.

entry.title: Hackmd.io
entry.description: Collaborative markdown editor for teams to write, share, and publish documents in real time.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan allows unlimited public notes and basic collaboration features.
caveats: Private notes and advanced features require paid plan
Team management limited on free tier

quickstartSteps: Go to https://hackmd.io/ and sign up with email or OAuth provider.
Create a new note and start writing in markdown.
Share the note link to collaborate in real time.
Organize notes into folders or teams as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Hackmd.io for quick, collaborative markdown editing and sharing with teams or open communities.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced access control, unlimited private notes, or strict on-premise hosting.
entry.body: Hackmd.io offers a free, always-available collaborative markdown editor with unlimited public notes and basic sharing, ideal for teams and open communities.

entry.title: Meet.jit.si
entry.description: Meet.jit.si offers free, browser-based video conferencing with no account required and unlimited meeting duration.

freeTierDetails.summary: Unlimited free video meetings with no registration required.
caveats: No SLA or guaranteed uptime
No custom branding
No meeting recording on hosted version

quickstartSteps: Go to https://meet.jit.si/ in your browser.
Enter a unique meeting name and click 'Start meeting'.
Allow browser access to your camera and microphone.
Share the meeting link with participants.
Join the call and use features like screen sharing or chat.
entry.whenToUse: Use Meet.jit.si for instant, free video meetings without sign-up or installation, especially for small teams or casual calls.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need enterprise features like managed user accounts, meeting recordings, or guaranteed uptime.
entry.body: Meet.jit.si provides free, open-source video conferencing directly in your browser with no registration, making it ideal for quick, privacy-friendly meetings.

entry.title: Mintlify
entry.description: Mintlify offers tools to create, host, and maintain beautiful, interactive documentation for your codebase.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free tier for public documentation sites with core features.
caveats: Private docs and advanced integrations require paid plans
Branding removal not included

quickstartSteps: Sign up at mintlify.com with your GitHub account.
Connect your documentation repository or create a new one.
Configure your docs with the provided Mintlify template.
Deploy your documentation site with one click.
Share your public docs link with your users.
entry.whenToUse: Use Mintlify when you need a fast, attractive way to publish public documentation for your codebase or open source project.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require private documentation, custom integrations, or advanced analytics without upgrading to a paid plan.
entry.body: Mintlify provides a simple, modern platform for hosting and maintaining public documentation sites, ideal for open source and small teams seeking easy setup.

entry.title: Notion
entry.description: Notion is an all-in-one workspace for notes, docs, wikis, and project management with real-time collaboration.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan includes unlimited pages, blocks, and up to 10 guests.
limits: File uploads up to 5MB per file
caveats: Advanced permissions and admin tools require paid plans
File upload size is limited

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Notion account at https://www.notion.so/
Create a new workspace or join an existing one.
Start a new page and choose a template or create from scratch.
Invite collaborators by email if needed.
Organize pages into folders or databases as required.
entry.whenToUse: Use Notion for organizing notes, wikis, and lightweight project management with easy sharing and collaboration.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Notion if you need advanced admin controls, offline-first access, or handle large files frequently.
entry.body: Notion provides a flexible workspace for notes, docs, and lightweight project management, suitable for individuals and teams seeking easy collaboration and organization.

entry.title: Nuclino
entry.description: Nuclino is a collaborative workspace for teams to organize knowledge, manage projects, and share documents in real time.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan for up to 50 items and 2GB storage per workspace.
caveats: No advanced permissions
Limited integrations

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Nuclino account.
Create a new workspace for your team or project.
Invite collaborators via email.
Start adding items (notes, docs, tasks) to your workspace.
Organize content using clusters and links.
entry.whenToUse: Use Nuclino when you need a simple, real-time wiki or collaborative workspace for small teams or projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced permissions, unlimited storage, or deep integrations with external tools.
entry.body: Nuclino offers a lightweight, collaborative workspace ideal for small teams to organize knowledge, manage projects, and document processes with ease.

entry.title: React Documentation
entry.description: Official documentation for learning, referencing, and mastering React for building user interfaces.

freeTierDetails.summary: Completely free and open access to all React documentation.

quickstartSteps: Visit https://react.dev.
Read the 'Quick Start' guide on the homepage.
Follow the interactive tutorials or code examples.
Explore API reference and advanced guides as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use when you need official, up-to-date guidance or reference for building React applications.
entry.whenNotToUse: Not suitable if you require backend, deployment, or non-React framework documentation.
entry.body: React Documentation provides comprehensive, official resources for learning and referencing React, including guides, tutorials, and API documentation for all experience levels.

entry.title: Revolt.chat
entry.description: Revolt.chat is an open-source chat platform for communities and teams, offering free self-hosting and public cloud options.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free public cloud instance and unlimited self-hosting with no usage limits.
limits: No enforced user or message limits
Public instance subject to fair use
caveats: Public instance may have moderation or fair use restrictions
No official SLA for public cloud

quickstartSteps: Sign up at https://app.revolt.chat/ or download the desktop/mobile app.
Create or join a server to start chatting.
Invite others to your server using the invite link.
For self-hosting, follow the official guide to deploy your own instance.
entry.whenToUse: Use Revolt.chat for free, open-source chat hosting for communities or teams, especially if you want self-hosting or Discord-like features.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require enterprise support, advanced integrations, or guaranteed uptime for mission-critical production workloads.
entry.body: Revolt.chat provides a free, open-source alternative to mainstream chat platforms, supporting both public cloud and self-hosted deployments for communities and teams.

entry.title: Rocket.Chat
entry.description: Rocket.Chat is an open-source team communication platform offering chat, video, and file sharing for organizations.

freeTierDetails.summary: Community Edition is free for unlimited users and messages with self-hosting.
caveats: Cloud-hosted plans have user and feature limits
Enterprise features require paid plan
billingRiskNotes: No billing for self-hosted Community Edition

quickstartSteps: Download the latest Rocket.Chat Community Edition from the official website.
Install Rocket.Chat on your server using Docker, Snap, or manual installation.
Configure environment variables and database connection (MongoDB required).
Start the Rocket.Chat server and access the web interface.
Create your admin account and invite team members.
entry.whenToUse: Use Rocket.Chat when you need a customizable, self-hosted chat solution for teams or communities with full data control.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require managed hosting, advanced enterprise features, or official support without a paid plan.
entry.body: Rocket.Chat provides a robust, open-source alternative to proprietary chat platforms, ideal for organizations seeking control, extensibility, and privacy in their communications.

entry.title: Substack
entry.description: Substack lets you publish newsletters and monetize email subscriptions with a simple, no-code platform.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free to start publishing and sending newsletters; Substack charges a fee only when you enable paid subscriptions.
caveats: Substack takes 10% of paid subscription revenue
Stripe processing fees apply for paid subscriptions

useCases: Launching a personal or company newsletter
quickstartSteps: Sign up at substack.com with your email.
Choose a publication name and customize your profile.
Import existing subscribers or start with none.
Write your first post and send it to your audience.
Optionally, enable paid subscriptions to monetize.
entry.whenToUse: Use Substack when you want to quickly launch a newsletter and optionally monetize your audience without technical setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need advanced customization, full control over branding, or integration with external email marketing tools.
entry.body: Substack is a popular platform for writers and creators to publish newsletters, manage subscribers, and monetize content with minimal technical overhead.

entry.title: Telegram
entry.description: Telegram is a cloud-based messaging platform offering free group chats, channels, bots, and file sharing.

freeTierDetails.summary: Telegram is free for unlimited messaging, groups, channels, and bots.
caveats: API rate limits apply for bots
No guaranteed uptime or SLA

quickstartSteps: Download and install the Telegram app or use the web version.
Sign up with your phone number and verify your account.
Create a group, channel, or start a chat.
Access BotFather to create and configure a bot if needed.
Invite users or share your group/channel link.
entry.whenToUse: Use Telegram for free, secure group messaging, broadcasting, or building chatbots with minimal setup.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need enterprise-grade compliance, advanced moderation, or guaranteed uptime/SLA.
entry.body: Telegram offers free, unlimited messaging, groups, channels, and bot APIs, making it ideal for communities, teams, and lightweight automation.

entry.title: Twist.com
entry.description: Twist.com is a team communication platform focused on organized, asynchronous messaging for distributed teams.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan includes unlimited users, 1 month of message history, and up to 5 integrations.
caveats: Older messages are archived after 1 month.
Limited storage may restrict large file sharing.

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Twist.com account.
Create a team and invite members via email.
Set up channels for different topics or projects.
Start threads to organize conversations.
Connect up to 5 integrations as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Twist.com when you need organized, asynchronous team communication with minimal chat distractions.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require unlimited message history, advanced integrations, or real-time chat features similar to Slack.
entry.body: Twist.com offers a structured, asynchronous messaging platform ideal for teams seeking focused communication and reduced chat overload, with a generous free plan for small groups.

entry.title: Webex
entry.description: Webex provides video conferencing, online meetings, and team collaboration tools for remote and hybrid teams.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan includes unlimited meetings up to 40 minutes and 100 participants per meeting.
caveats: Meetings automatically end after 40 minutes
Some advanced features require paid plans

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Webex account at the official website.
Verify your email and log in to the Webex dashboard.
Schedule a new meeting or start an instant meeting.
Invite participants by sharing the meeting link.
Join the meeting using the Webex app or browser.
entry.whenToUse: Use Webex for reliable video meetings, webinars, or team collaboration with up to 100 participants and basic messaging features.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need meetings longer than 40 minutes, advanced integrations, or large-scale webinars without upgrading to a paid plan.
entry.body: Webex offers a robust free plan for video meetings and collaboration, suitable for small teams, educators, and professionals needing secure, reliable conferencing.

entry.title: Whereby.com
entry.description: Whereby.com provides browser-based video meetings with no downloads or logins required for guests.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan includes unlimited 1:1 meetings and limited group meetings.
caveats: Group meetings are limited to 45 minutes
Some features require paid plans

quickstartSteps: Go to whereby.com and sign up for a free account.
Create your personal meeting room.
Share your meeting link with participants.
Start your video meeting directly in the browser.
entry.whenToUse: Use Whereby when you need fast, browser-based video meetings without requiring guests to install software or create accounts.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Whereby if you need unlimited group meeting durations, advanced moderation, or deep integrations with enterprise tools.
entry.body: Whereby.com offers simple, browser-based video meetings with a free plan suitable for individuals and small teams needing quick, no-fuss calls.

entry.title: Zoho Docs
entry.description: Zoho Docs is a cloud-based document management and collaboration platform for storing, sharing, and editing files online.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan includes basic document storage and collaboration features for up to 5 users.
caveats: Advanced admin controls and integrations require paid plans
Limited support on free tier

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Zoho Docs account.
Verify your email address and log in.
Create or upload your first document or file.
Invite team members to collaborate.
Organize files into folders as needed.
entry.whenToUse: Use Zoho Docs when you need simple, cloud-based document storage and collaboration for small teams or personal projects.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced integrations, large-scale enterprise features, or unlimited storage.
entry.body: Zoho Docs offers a free, user-friendly platform for storing, sharing, and collaborating on documents online, ideal for individuals and small teams.

entry.title: Zoho Wiki
entry.description: Zoho Wiki is a collaborative online knowledge management and documentation tool for teams and organizations.

freeTierDetails.summary: Zoho Wiki offers a free plan for up to 3 users with basic features.
caveats: Limited storage and user count
No advanced integrations

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a Zoho account.
Navigate to Zoho Wiki and create a new wiki.
Invite up to 2 additional users.
Start adding pages and organizing your content.
entry.whenToUse: Use Zoho Wiki when you need a simple, collaborative wiki for small teams or groups with basic documentation needs.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you require advanced integrations, large storage, or support for more than 3 users on the free plan.
entry.body: Zoho Wiki provides a straightforward platform for small teams to collaboratively create and manage documentation, with a free plan suitable for basic needs.

entry.title: Zoom.us
entry.description: Zoom.us provides cloud-based video conferencing, online meetings, and collaboration tools for individuals and organizations.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan allows unlimited 1:1 meetings and group meetings up to 40 minutes.
caveats: Group meetings end after 40 minutes
Limited cloud recording
Some advanced features require paid plans

quickstartSteps: Sign up for a free Zoom account at https://zoom.us/signup.
Verify your email and log in to your Zoom dashboard.
Download and install the Zoom desktop or mobile app.
Schedule or start a new meeting from the dashboard.
Invite participants using the meeting link or email invitation.
entry.whenToUse: Use Zoom.us for quick, reliable video meetings or webinars with up to 100 participants and basic collaboration features.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid if you need group meetings longer than 40 minutes for free, advanced admin controls, or full cloud recording.
entry.body: Zoom.us offers a robust free tier for video meetings, making it suitable for individuals and small teams needing reliable online collaboration. The 40-minute group meeting limit is a key constraint, but unlimited 1:1 meetings and up to 100 participants per session make it practical for most basic needs. Paid plans unlock longer meetings, advanced features, and more administrative controls.

entry.title: Zulip
entry.description: Zulip is an open-source team chat platform combining real-time and threaded messaging for organized collaboration.

freeTierDetails.summary: Free plan for up to 100 users with unlimited message history and integrations.
caveats: No SAML/SSO on free tier
No priority support

quickstartSteps: Sign up at zulip.com with your email address.
Create a new organization or join an existing one.
Invite team members via email or share an invite link.
Set up channels (streams) for different topics or projects.
Install desktop or mobile apps for easier access.
entry.whenToUse: Use Zulip when you need organized, topic-based team chat with unlimited history and open-source flexibility.
entry.whenNotToUse: Avoid Zulip if you require enterprise features like SAML/SSO or advanced admin controls on the free plan.
entry.body: Zulip offers a modern, open-source chat platform with threaded conversations, making it easy to organize team discussions and collaborate efficiently.
